verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I TM protein with primary localization to ER-Golgi, but documented evidence shows a functional PM pool that cycles between compartments as part of its cargo-receptor cycling. The protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the outer leaflet during vesicular trafficking and in maintaining surface-delivered cargo. This dual localization with trafficking cycling to the PM qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via the dual_localization mechanism.
